entity.summary: Long-running centralized cryptocurrency exchange launched in the United States and later operated as a global platform after ownership changes. Poloniex suffered a major hot-wallet compromise on November 10, 2023, temporarily suspended deposits and withdrawals, and began restoring those services on November 30, 2023.
entity.notes: The United States field reflects Poloniex's historical origin. Circle acquired the exchange in 2018 and spun it out in 2019 to a new international operator; those ownership events do not imply a terminal shutdown. The 2023 security incident caused a temporary transfer-service suspension, followed by staged restoration.

expected.summary: United States-based centralized exchange launched in 2014 and still operating under the Poloniex brand.
expected.notes: Poloniex acquisition and later Circle spinout are modeled as ownership events rather than terminal death-side status.

changes.summary: Long-running centralized cryptocurrency exchange launched in the United States and later operated as a global platform after ownership changes. Poloniex suffered a major hot-wallet compromise on November 10, 2023, temporarily suspended deposits and withdrawals, and began restoring those services on November 30, 2023.
changes.notes: The United States field reflects Poloniex's historical origin. Circle acquired the exchange in 2018 and spun it out in 2019 to a new international operator; those ownership events do not imply a terminal shutdown. The 2023 security incident caused a temporary transfer-service suspension, followed by staged restoration.

events.title: Poloniex hot wallets were compromised
events.description: Poloniex suffered a major hot-wallet security incident involving a private-key compromise and losses reported above USD 100 million across multiple networks.
events.notes: Loss estimates varied by source and on-chain accounting; the event description therefore uses a conservative above-USD-100-million formulation.

events.title: Deposits and withdrawals were suspended after the hack
events.description: Poloniex temporarily suspended deposit and withdrawal services while it investigated the hot-wallet compromise and upgraded wallet and security infrastructure.
events.notes: Trading resumed sooner than transfer services; this event is scoped to deposits and withdrawals.

events.title: Poloniex began restoring deposit and withdrawal services
events.description: Poloniex announced that deposit and withdrawal services would resume from November 30, 2023 following nearly twenty days of suspension and security remediation.
events.notes: Restoration was staged and asset support continued to expand after the initial reopening marker.

evidence.title: Poloniex to Gradually Resume Trading Services
evidence.notes: First-party acknowledgement of the hacking incident and recovery work.

evidence.title: Poloniex Incident Analysis
evidence.notes: Technical incident analysis identifying a hot-wallet private-key compromise and the scale of losses.

evidence.title: Poloniex to Gradually Resume Trading Services
evidence.notes: First-party recovery notice stating that deposit and withdrawal services were still being upgraded after the incident.

evidence.title: Poloniex to Gradually Resume Deposit and Withdrawal Services and Launch Airdrop
evidence.notes: First-party notice stating that the hack required a nearly twenty-day suspension of deposits and withdrawals.

evidence.title: Poloniex to Gradually Resume Deposit and Withdrawal Services and Launch Airdrop
evidence.notes: First-party announcement setting November 30, 2023 as the initial service-restoration marker.

evidence.title: Poloniex official exchange platform
evidence.notes: Current official domain confirming continued exchange operation.
